notes: This file is the hand-curated source of truth for text, printed line grouping, and playback metadata.
Only entries marked verified should be turned into production audio.

pages.title: Blessing 1 intro and exercise
pages.englishText: Blessing 1: The Blessing over the Fruit of the Vine. The blessing begins. Read the Hebrew from right to left. The first letter of our blessing is Bet, which has the sound of B as in Berachah. Locate the bet throughout this paragraph from the siddur, prayer book. Pronounce the B sound each time in order to reinforce recognition.
notes: Page 1 is split into an intro section and an exercise section.
Only the exercise section currently uses the active scanned-page highlight.
Page-level English playback reads only the printed English on the page, not a translation of the Hebrew prayer lines.
Printed divine-name abbreviations remain visible as written but are spoken as Adonai.

sections.title: Intro section
sections.description: Top-of-page cues, reading direction, and the bet-recognition setup.
sections.mixedText: Blessing 1: The Blessing over the Fruit of the Vine. The blessing begins: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה אֲדֹנָי. Read the Hebrew from right to left: בָּרוּךְ. The first letter of our blessing is ב, בֵּית, which has the sound of B as in בְּרָכָה. Locate the ב throughout this paragraph from the סִדּוּר, prayer book. Pronounce the B sound each time in order to reinforce recognition.

pages.title: Kamatz practice
notes: Printed page 2 in the book sequence.
The intro is played as one mixed block; the exercise row is split into word buttons.

sections.title: Intro section
sections.description: Top-of-page explanation for the kamatz vowel.
sections.mixedText: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה אֲדֹנָי. In Hebrew, vowels are written above, below, before, or after the consonant. The first vowel, found under the בּ, is קָמַץ. It has the sound of ah as in שַׁבָּת. Hebrew is read in an orderly manner: consonant, vowel, consonant, vowel, consonant, vowel, etc. When you combine the consonant בּ and the vowel קָמַץ, the resulting sound is בָּ, bah. Read this line.

sections.mixedText: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה אֲדֹנָי. רֵישׁ has the sound of R as in רֹאשׁ הַשָּׁנָה. Read these sounds.

sections.title: Intro section
sections.description: Top-of-page explanation for the shuruk vowel.
sections.mixedText: בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה אֲדֹנָי. שׁוּרוּק is a vowel which has the sound of oo or u as in שָׁבוּעוֹת.
